data.name: HostDeploymentAssignments.Lede
data.value: Assignments connect hosts to deployment planning. This is intentionally separated from the main manual admin workflow.

data.name: HostDeployments.Lede
data.value: Inspect HostAgent app deployments, artifact provisioning and explicit template rollout requests.

data.name: Principal is already assigned to role.
data.value: Principal is already assigned to role.

data.name: Roles are the main RBAC editing surface. Add permissions and principals here so access remains grouped and auditable.
data.value: Roles are the main security editing surface. Add permissions and principals here so access remains grouped and auditable.

data.name: Workers combines app worker definitions with the live worker runtime so you can manage the worker track from one place.
data.value: Workers combines app worker definitions with the live worker runtime so you can manage the worker track from one place.

data.name: Manage your OMP user profile and portal defaults.
data.value: Manage your OMP user profile and portal defaults.

data.name: Display name must be 200 characters or fewer.
data.value: Display name must be 200 characters or fewer.

data.name: Manage OMP users and their authentication links. Users can exist before they are linked to an AD account.
data.value: Manage OMP users and their authentication links. Users can exist before they are linked to an AD account.

data.name: No OMP users have been created yet.
data.value: No OMP users have been created yet.

data.name: Create an OMP user and optionally add a local password login immediately. AD authentication links can still be added from the edit page.
data.value: Create an OMP user and optionally add a local password login immediately. AD authentication links can still be added from the edit page.

data.name: Create an OMP user and optionally add AD or local password authentication immediately.
data.value: Create an OMP user and optionally add AD or local password authentication immediately.

data.name: User created with AD link and local login.
data.value: User created with AD link and local login.

data.name: Create a local password login now by entering a user name and password. Leave these fields empty to create only the OMP user.
data.value: Create a local password login now by entering a user name and password. Leave these fields empty to create only the OMP user.

data.name: Update the OMP user record and link AD identities that Windows sign-in can resolve.
data.value: Update the OMP user record and link AD identities that Windows sign-in can resolve.

data.name: These provider identities are linked to this OMP user. They are shown read-only in this version.
data.value: These provider identities are linked to this OMP user. They are shown read-only in this version.

data.name: These provider identities are linked to this OMP user. Removing all links prevents sign-in until another link is added.
data.value: These provider identities are linked to this OMP user. Removing all links prevents sign-in until another link is added.

data.name: This OMP user has no auth links.
data.value: This OMP user has no auth links.

data.name: There are {0} direct AD-user role assignments that can be moved to this OMP user.
data.value: There are {0} direct AD-user role assignments that can be moved to this OMP user.

data.name: Move AD rights to OMP user
data.value: Move AD rights to OMP user

data.name: No direct AD-user rights are available to move.
data.value: No direct AD-user rights are available to move.

data.name: Moved {0} direct AD-user role assignments to this OMP user. Created {1} OMP-user role assignments.
data.value: Moved {0} direct AD-user role assignments to this OMP user. Created {1} OMP-user role assignments.

data.name: This OMP user has no AD links.
data.value: This OMP user has no AD links.

data.name: The AD rights could not be moved.
data.value: The AD rights could not be moved.

data.name: Use the same AD key format that Windows sign-in resolves, for example DOMAIN\username, name:DOMAIN\username or sid:S-1-5-21-...
data.value: Use the same AD key format that Windows sign-in resolves, for example DOMAIN\username, name:DOMAIN\username or sid:S-1-5-21-...

data.name: Enter an AD provider user key.
data.value: Enter an AD provider user key.

data.name: AD provider user key must be 1000 characters or fewer.
data.value: AD provider user key must be 1000 characters or fewer.

data.name: This AD account is already linked to this user.
data.value: This AD account is already linked to this user.

data.name: This AD account is already linked to user {0}.
data.value: This AD account is already linked to user {0}.

data.name: The AD authentication provider is missing.
data.value: The AD authentication provider is missing.

data.name: The AD account could not be linked.
data.value: The AD account could not be linked.

data.name: You are signed in with Windows/AD, but this sign-in is not linked to an OMP user account yet.
data.value: You are signed in with Windows/AD, but this sign-in is not linked to an OMP user account yet.

data.name: Create an OMP account to save profile settings and link this AD sign-in to your account.
data.value: Create an OMP account to save profile settings and link this AD sign-in to your account.

data.name: The current sign-in is not an AD sign-in that can be linked.
data.value: The current sign-in is not an AD sign-in that can be linked.

data.name: The AD authentication provider is not available.
data.value: The AD authentication provider is not available.

data.name: This AD account is already linked to an OMP user. Sign out and sign in again.
data.value: This AD account is already linked to an OMP user. Sign out and sign in again.

data.name: The OMP user account could not be created.
data.value: The OMP user account could not be created.

data.name: The local password could not be reset.
data.value: The local password could not be reset.

data.name: The auth link could not be removed.
data.value: The auth link could not be removed.

data.name: Add a local password login linked to this OMP user.
data.value: Add a local password login linked to this OMP user.

data.name: User name is already in use.
data.value: User name is already in use.

data.name: The local password authentication provider is missing or disabled.
data.value: The local password authentication provider is missing or disabled.

data.name: The local login could not be added.
data.value: The local login could not be added.

data.name: User name must be 256 characters or fewer.
data.value: User name must be 256 characters or fewer.

data.name: Password must be at least 8 characters.
data.value: Password must be at least 8 characters.

data.name: Password and confirmation password do not match.
data.value: Password and confirmation password do not match.

data.name: Bind OMP users or AD users to the role. AD group assignment is not available yet.
data.value: Bind OMP users or AD users to the role. AD group assignment is not available yet.

data.name: For OMP users, start typing a display name or user ID and choose a suggestion. For AD users, start typing an existing AD user principal or enter DOMAIN\\User.
data.value: For OMP users, start typing a display name or user ID and choose a suggestion. For AD users, start typing an existing AD user principal or enter DOMAIN\\User.

data.name: AD group assignment is not available yet.
data.value: AD group assignment is not available yet.

data.name: Select an OMP user from the suggestions or enter a valid user ID.
data.value: Select an OMP user from the suggestions or enter a valid user ID.

data.name: This AD account is already linked to an OMP user. Assign the role to the OMP user instead.
data.value: This AD account is already linked to an OMP user. Assign the role to the OMP user instead.

data.name: Portal entries are curated start points for the destinations you can access.
data.value: Portal entries are curated start points for the destinations you can access.

data.name: Create global Portal Entries that appear on the Portal start page for users with access to the target.
data.value: Create global Portal Entries that appear on the Portal start page for users with access to the target.

data.name: Use a local path such as /content/ex2, a relative path such as content/ex2, or an absolute http/https URL.
data.value: Use a local path such as /content/ex2, a relative path such as content/ex2, or an absolute http/https URL.

data.name: Optional stable navigation entry key. Leave empty when target URL is enough.
data.value: Optional stable navigation entry key. Leave empty when target URL is enough.

data.name: Enter a target URL or target entry key.
data.value: Enter a target URL or target entry key.

data.name: Use an absolute http/https URL, a local path starting with /, or a relative path.
data.value: Use an absolute http/https URL, a local path starting with /, or a relative path.

data.name: Update the Portal Entry shown on the Portal start page.
data.value: Update the Portal Entry shown on the Portal start page.

data.name: This entry is seeded from an app instance. The app target is resolved before custom target fields.
data.value: This entry is seeded from an app instance. The app target is resolved before custom target fields.

data.name: Portal setting definitions are installed by the Portal. This page stores only values that override defaults for this user.
data.value: Portal setting definitions are installed by the Portal. This page stores only values that override defaults for this user.

data.name: Use an integer for int-backed settings. Use text for string-backed settings. Saving the default value removes the user override.
data.value: Use an integer for int-backed settings. Use text for string-backed settings. Saving the default value removes the user override.

data.name: No portal setting values have been configured for this user.
data.value: No portal setting values have been configured for this user.

data.name: This portal setting is not available.
data.value: This portal setting is not available.

data.name: This portal setting uses an unsupported value type.
data.value: This portal setting uses an unsupported value type.

data.name: A value for this portal setting already exists for this user.
data.value: A value for this portal setting already exists for this user.

data.name: Choose which Portal Entries are visible in the topbar and adjust the parent/child layout. Drop a leaf entry on a top-level entry to make it a child.
data.value: Choose which Portal Entries are visible in the topbar and adjust the parent/child layout. Drop a leaf entry on a top-level entry to make it a child.

data.name: Layout parent entry was not found.
data.value: Layout parent entry was not found.

data.name: A Portal Entry cannot be its own parent.
data.value: A Portal Entry cannot be its own parent.

data.name: The layout update would create a Portal Entry cycle.
data.value: The layout update would create a Portal Entry cycle.

data.name: Leave empty for a top-level Portal Entry. Choose a parent to create a child link in the top bar.
data.value: Leave empty for a top-level Portal Entry. Choose a parent to create a child link in the top bar.

data.name: The selected parent would create a Portal Entry cycle.
data.value: The selected parent would create a Portal Entry cycle.

data.name: The admin area is organized around one OMP installation. Use Installation for desired topology, Hosts for concrete machines, Apps for deployable components and System for configuration and access.
data.value: The admin area is organized around one OMP installation. Use Installation for desired topology, Hosts for concrete machines, Apps for deployable components and System for configuration and access.

data.name: The admin area is organized around one OMP installation. Use Installation for desired topology, System for artifacts and deployment status, and Administration for configuration and access.
data.value: The admin area is organized around one OMP installation. Use Installation for desired topology, System for artifacts and deployment status, and Administration for configuration and access.

data.name: These pages are the main workflow when setting up and maintaining the OMP installation.
data.value: These pages are the main workflow when setting up and maintaining the OMP installation.

data.name: HostAgent follows the desired installation state and records deployment attempts for review.
data.value: HostAgent follows the desired installation state and records deployment attempts for review.

data.name: HostAgent follows the desired installation state, deploys artifact versions and records deployment attempts for review.
data.value: HostAgent follows the desired installation state, deploys artifact versions and records deployment attempts for review.

data.name: Automation pages now live under System so the admin flow follows the current model: one installation, concrete hosts, deployable apps and HostAgent status.
data.value: Automation pages now live under System so the admin flow follows the current model: one installation, concrete hosts, deployable apps and HostAgent status.

data.name: Hosts are the concrete machines or runtime targets inside the OMP installation. HostAgent reports and deploys apps against these host keys.
data.value: Hosts are the concrete machines or runtime targets inside the OMP installation. HostAgent reports and deploys apps against these host keys.

data.name: Optional files that HostAgent writes after deploying this artifact. Use this for site-specific runtime configuration that should not be packaged in the artifact zip.
data.value: Optional files that HostAgent writes after deploying this artifact. Use this for site-specific runtime configuration that should not be packaged in the artifact zip.

data.name: No configuration files are defined for this artifact.
data.value: No configuration files are defined for this artifact.

data.name: HostAgent writes enabled configuration files after deploying the artifact so site-specific settings can change without rebuilding the zip package.
data.value: HostAgent writes enabled configuration files after deploying the artifact so site-specific settings can change without rebuilding the zip package.

data.name: Path relative to the deployed app root, for example odv.site.config.js.
data.value: Path relative to the deployed app root, for example odv.site.config.js.

data.name: HostAgent writes this text as UTF-8 without BOM and overwrites the file when the deployed copy differs.
data.value: HostAgent writes this text as UTF-8 without BOM and overwrites the file when the deployed copy differs.

data.name: Upload a text file to replace the stored configuration content without posting the file body as a textarea field.
data.value: Upload a text file to replace the stored configuration content without posting the file body as a textarea field.

data.name: Upload a text file or a zip file containing exactly one text file. Zip upload is useful when request filters block script-like text content.
data.value: Upload a text file or a zip file containing exactly one text file. Zip upload is useful when request filters block script-like text content.

data.name: HostAgent writes uploaded text as UTF-8 without BOM and overwrites the deployed file when the deployed copy differs.
data.value: HostAgent writes uploaded text as UTF-8 without BOM and overwrites the deployed file when the deployed copy differs.

data.name: Use this only when the environment allows posting this content as a form field. File upload is safer behind strict request filters.
data.value: Use this only when the environment allows posting this content as a form field. File upload is safer behind strict request filters.

data.name: Delete this artifact configuration file? HostAgent will stop managing the file, but existing deployed files are not removed.
data.value: Delete this artifact configuration file? HostAgent will stop managing the file, but existing deployed files are not removed.

data.name: Deleting this row does not delete files that have already been written on hosts.
data.value: Deleting this row does not delete files that have already been written on hosts.

data.name: Artifact configuration file was not found.
data.value: Artifact configuration file was not found.

data.name: The uploaded configuration file is too large. The maximum size is 1048576 bytes.
data.value: The uploaded configuration file is too large. The maximum size is 1048576 bytes.

data.name: The uploaded configuration file must be valid UTF-8 text.
data.value: The uploaded configuration file must be valid UTF-8 text.

data.name: The uploaded zip file must contain exactly one UTF-8 text file.
data.value: The uploaded zip file must contain exactly one UTF-8 text file.

data.name: Relative path must stay below the deployed app root and must not contain rooted paths or parent directory segments.
data.value: Relative path must stay below the deployed app root and must not contain rooted paths or parent directory segments.

data.name: This artifact already has a configuration file with the same relative path.
data.value: This artifact already has a configuration file with the same relative path.

data.name: The artifact configuration file could not be saved.
data.value: The artifact configuration file could not be saved.

data.name: The artifact configuration file could not be deleted.
data.value: The artifact configuration file could not be deleted.

data.name: An artifact with identical extracted content already exists: {0} {1} ({2}).
data.value: An artifact with identical extracted content already exists: {0} {1} ({2}).

data.name: An artifact with the same app, package type, target, version, and content already exists. No upload is needed.
data.value: An artifact with the same app, package type, target, version, and content already exists. No upload is needed.

data.name: An artifact with the same app, package type, target, and version already exists, but the uploaded content is different. Confirm replacement to overwrite the existing artifact content.
data.value: An artifact with the same app, package type, target, and version already exists, but the uploaded content is different. Confirm replacement to overwrite the existing artifact content.

data.name: Existing artifact content replaced and metadata updated.
data.value: Existing artifact content replaced and metadata updated.

data.name: Existing artifact configuration file rows were preserved.
data.value: Existing artifact configuration file rows were preserved.

data.name: Replace existing artifact with same identity
data.value: Replace existing artifact with same identity

data.name: Replace existing dashboard widgets for rollback or repair
data.value: Replace existing dashboard widgets for rollback or repair

data.name: Only use this when the same app, package type, target, and version already exists and the uploaded content should intentionally replace it. Automatic import never performs this replacement.
data.value: Only use this when the same app, package type, target, and version already exists and the uploaded content should intentionally replace it. Automatic import never performs this replacement.

data.name: The filename references module '{0}' and app '{1}', but that enabled app definition was not found. Register the module/app first or select an existing app.
data.value: The filename references module '{0}' and app '{1}', but that enabled app definition was not found. Register the module/app first or select an existing app.

data.name: Do not include runtime configuration files such as appsettings*.json or odv.site.config.js in the artifact zip. HostAgent writes those from deployment configuration or artifact configuration file rows.
data.value: Do not include runtime configuration files such as appsettings*.json or odv.site.config.js in the artifact zip. HostAgent writes those from deployment configuration or artifact configuration file rows.

data.name: Copy configuration files from previous version
data.value: Copy configuration files from previous version

data.name: Copies artifact configuration file rows from the latest previous artifact with the same app, package type, and target.
data.value: Copies artifact configuration file rows from the latest previous artifact with the same app, package type, and target.

data.name: No previous artifact configuration files were found to copy.
data.value: No previous artifact configuration files were found to copy.

data.name: Copied {0} configuration file(s) from artifact version {1}.
data.value: Copied {0} configuration file(s) from artifact version {1}.

data.name: Configuration files could not be copied automatically. Add them from the artifact edit page.
data.value: Configuration files could not be copied automatically. Add them from the artifact edit page.

data.name: Legacy artifact zips must not include runtime configuration files such as appsettings*.json or odv.site.config.js. Use an OMP artifact package manifest when configuration files should be registered together with the artifact.
data.value: Legacy artifact zips must not include runtime configuration files such as appsettings*.json or odv.site.config.js. Use an OMP artifact package manifest when configuration files should be registered together with the artifact.

data.name: Copies artifact configuration file rows from the latest previous artifact with the same app, package type, and target when the uploaded package does not declare configuration files.
data.value: Copies artifact configuration file rows from the latest previous artifact with the same app, package type, and target when the uploaded package does not declare configuration files.

data.name: Configuration files from the artifact package could not be saved. Review the artifact edit page before deploying this version.
data.value: Configuration files from the artifact package could not be saved. Review the artifact edit page before deploying this version.

data.name: Registered {0} configuration file(s) from the artifact package.
data.value: Registered {0} configuration file(s) from the artifact package.

data.name: Choose a host for runtime apps. Leave host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer.
data.value: Choose a host for runtime apps. Leave host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer.

data.name: Choose a template host for runtime apps. Leave host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer.
data.value: Choose a template host for runtime apps. Leave host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer.

data.name: Only one active desired app row can exist for the selected app and host placement.
data.value: Only one active desired app row can exist for the selected app and host placement.

data.name: An app instance with the same key or active host placement already exists.
data.value: An app instance with the same key or active host placement already exists.

data.name: A desired app with the same key or active host placement already exists.
data.value: A desired app with the same key or active host placement already exists.

data.name: Leave host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer. Runtime apps and non-load-balanced web apps should choose one host.
data.value: Leave host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer. Runtime apps and non-load-balanced web apps should choose one host.

data.name: Leave template host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer. Runtime apps and non-load-balanced web apps should choose one template host.
data.value: Leave template host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer. Runtime apps and non-load-balanced web apps should choose one template host.

data.name: Updates matching desired app rows so HostAgent starts deploying this artifact on its next cycle.
data.value: Updates matching desired app rows so HostAgent starts deploying this artifact on its next cycle.

data.name: Selected this artifact as desired version for {0} matching app row(s).
data.value: Selected this artifact as desired version for {0} matching app row(s).

data.name: The artifact could not be selected as the desired version automatically. Choose it from the installation template page.
data.value: The artifact could not be selected as the desired version automatically. Choose it from the installation template page.

data.name: When this is turned off, menus open only by click or keyboard shortcut.
data.value: When this is turned off, menus open only by click or keyboard shortcut.

data.name: When this is turned on, notification and message toasts are not shown.
data.value: When this is turned on, notification and message toasts are not shown.

data.name: Use this editor to create manifest-based artifact packages with a deployable payload and deployment-owned configuration files.
data.value: Use this editor to create manifest-based artifact packages with a deployable payload and deployment-owned configuration files.

data.name: Artifact metadata is incomplete and cannot be exported as a standard package.
data.value: Artifact metadata is incomplete and cannot be exported as a standard package.

data.name: Artifact payload folder was not found in the artifact store.
data.value: Artifact payload folder was not found in the artifact store.

data.name: Use only for app definitions that intentionally create several runtime rows, such as one worker row per channel.
data.value: Use only for app definitions that intentionally create several runtime rows, such as one worker row per channel.

data.name: Arrange widgets freely on your personal canvas.
data.value: Arrange widgets freely on your personal canvas.

data.name: No widgets have been added yet.
data.value: No widgets have been added yet.

data.name: Dashboard widgets require an OMP user.
data.value: Dashboard widgets require an OMP user.

data.name: Select a widget for details and preview.
data.value: Select a widget for details and preview.

data.name: This clears your current dashboard widgets. Continue?
data.value: This clears your current dashboard widgets. Continue?

data.name: Manage registered Portal dashboard widget definitions and availability.
data.value: Manage registered Portal dashboard widget definitions and availability.

data.name: Dashboard widget import and export is handled from the shared Export and import page.
data.value: Dashboard widget import and export is handled from the shared Export and import page.

data.name: Leave empty to clear the widget description.
data.value: Leave empty to clear the widget description.

data.name: Import and export portable Portal dashboard widget definitions as JSON files.
data.value: Import and export portable Portal dashboard widget definitions as JSON files.

data.name: Upload one dashboard widget JSON file. Matching widgets are updated by widgetKey; missing widgets are created.
data.value: Upload one dashboard widget JSON file. Matching widgets are updated by widgetKey; missing widgets are created.

data.name: The import replaces the widget definition and its permission restrictions for matching widgetKey. moduleKey is optional ownership metadata, not part of the identity.
data.value: The import replaces the widget definition and its permission restrictions for matching widgetKey. moduleKey is optional ownership metadata, not part of the identity.

data.name: Export all widgets or only the widgets owned by one module key.
data.value: Export all widgets or only the widgets owned by one module key.

data.name: Widget JSON uses a globally stable widgetKey so private module repositories can version widget definitions without depending on database IDs. moduleKey is optional ownership metadata for filtering and export. Use empty permissionNames and roleNames arrays when a widget should be available to every signed-in OMP user.
data.value: Widget JSON uses a globally stable widgetKey so private module repositories can version widget definitions without depending on database IDs. moduleKey is optional ownership metadata for filtering and export. Use empty permissionNames and roleNames arrays when a widget should be available to every signed-in OMP user.

data.name: These widget definitions are currently registered in this OMP installation.
data.value: These widget definitions are currently registered in this OMP installation.

data.name: Empty adjustable widget for simple content or custom images.
data.value: Empty adjustable widget for simple content or custom images.

data.name: Shows portal administration metrics for users with admin access.
data.value: Shows portal administration metrics for users with admin access.

data.name: Lists the signed-in user's favorite portal entries.
data.value: Lists the signed-in user's favorite portal entries.

data.name: Lists portal entries the signed-in user can access.
data.value: Lists portal entries the signed-in user can access.

data.name: Combines favorite portal entries and all accessible portal entries in one list.
data.value: Combines favorite portal entries and all accessible portal entries in one list.

data.name: Shows portal navigation groups and links as dashboard content.
data.value: Shows portal navigation groups and links as dashboard content.

data.name: Shows the signed-in user's available roles and active role.
data.value: Shows the signed-in user's available roles and active role.

data.name: Lists content pages the signed-in user can access.
data.value: Lists content pages the signed-in user can access.

data.name: Shows the current weekday, date, and week number.
data.value: Shows the current weekday, date, and week number.

data.name: Embeds the portal music player widget.
data.value: Embeds the portal music player widget.

data.name: Imported dashboard widgets. Created: {0}. Updated: {1}. Permission rows: {2}.
data.value: Imported dashboard widgets. Created: {0}. Updated: {1}. Permission rows: {2}.

data.name: Select one dashboard widget JSON file.
data.value: Select one dashboard widget JSON file.

data.name: The uploaded dashboard widget file must be a .json file.
data.value: The uploaded dashboard widget file must be a .json file.

data.name: The uploaded dashboard widget file exceeds the configured limit of {0} bytes.
data.value: The uploaded dashboard widget file exceeds the configured limit of {0} bytes.

data.name: Install or complete modules from the package library. This applies the module definition, runs safe SQL repairs, registers artifacts, and updates matching desired app versions.
data.value: Install or complete modules from the package library. This applies the module definition, runs safe SQL repairs, registers artifacts, and updates matching desired app versions.

data.name: The matrix checks module definitions against current OMP metadata, required database objects, declared module dependencies, and current artifact selections. Stored definitions can be applied from the action column.
data.value: The matrix checks module definitions against current OMP metadata, required database objects, declared module dependencies, and current artifact selections. Stored definitions can be applied from the action column.

data.name: Importing again keeps unchanged content, skips identical artifacts, and can still execute missing SQL repairs.
data.value: Importing again keeps unchanged content, skips identical artifacts, and can still execute missing SQL repairs.

data.name: Module definition was not applied because current artifact selections are incompatible.
data.value: Module definition was not applied because current artifact selections are incompatible.

data.name: Module definition applied. No executable SQL repairs were needed.
data.value: Module definition applied. No executable SQL repairs were needed.

data.name: Module definition applied. Executed {0} SQL repair script(s).
data.value: Module definition applied. Executed {0} SQL repair script(s).

data.name: Use this editor to create or update machine-specific bootstrap configuration JSON files for the installer.
data.value: Use this editor to create or update machine-specific bootstrap configuration JSON files for the installer.

data.name: Service identity repair was requested. HostAgent will apply it during its next cycle when credential automation is set to PortalAdminApproved.
data.value: Service identity repair was requested. HostAgent will apply it during its next cycle when credential automation is set to PortalAdminApproved.

data.name: Choose a specific host when only one named host should run this app.
data.value: Choose a specific host when only one named host should run this app.

data.name: Choose a host role when every host with that role should run this app. Leave both placement fields empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer.
data.value: Choose a host role when every host with that role should run this app. Leave both placement fields empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer.

data.name: Choose either one specific host or one host role, not both.
data.value: Choose either one specific host or one host role, not both.

data.name: Selected host role was not found.
data.value: Selected host role was not found.

data.name: Choose a host or host role for runtime apps. Leave both empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer.
data.value: Choose a host or host role for runtime apps. Leave both empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer.

data.name: The admin area is organized around one OMP installation. Use Installation for desired topology, Packages for portable objects, and Operations for HostAgent troubleshooting.
data.value: The admin area is organized around one OMP installation. Use Installation for desired topology, Packages for portable objects, and Operations for HostAgent troubleshooting.

data.name: These are the normal pages for setup, upgrade, import, export and troubleshooting.
data.value: These are the normal pages for setup, upgrade, import, export and troubleshooting.

data.name: Use these standalone-compatible editors when preparing module definitions, artifact packages or machine-specific installer profiles.
data.value: Use these standalone-compatible editors when preparing module definitions, artifact packages or machine-specific installer profiles.

data.name: Automation pages now live under System so the admin flow follows the current model: installation topology, package import/export, and HostAgent operations.
data.value: Automation pages now live under System so the admin flow follows the current model: installation topology, package import/export, and HostAgent operations.

data.name: Import, export and install portable module packages containing one module definition JSON and compatible artifact package zips.
data.value: Import, export and install portable module packages containing one module definition JSON and compatible artifact package zips.

data.name: Packages are the portable object boundary. Installation controls desired topology and Operations shows what HostAgent did with the selected objects.
data.value: Packages are the portable object boundary. Installation controls desired topology and Operations shows what HostAgent did with the selected objects.

data.name: These editors can also run as standalone HTML tools from the package tools folder.
data.value: These editors can also run as standalone HTML tools from the package tools folder.

data.name: Inspect HostAgent app deployments, artifact provisioning, runtime identity checks, and explicit installation materialization requests.
data.value: Inspect HostAgent app deployments, artifact provisioning, runtime identity checks, and explicit installation materialization requests.

data.name: Manage the shared All modules menu. Users choose Favorites from the same entries, and dashboard widgets reuse both concepts.
data.value: Manage the shared All modules menu. Users choose Favorites from the same entries, and dashboard widgets reuse both concepts.

data.name: Manage the shared All modules menu. Users pick their own favorites from these entries.
data.value: Manage the shared All modules menu. Users pick their own favorites from these entries.

data.name: Choose which entries are visible in All modules and adjust the parent-child layout. Drop a leaf entry on a top-level entry to make it a child.
data.value: Choose which entries are visible in All modules and adjust the parent-child layout. Drop a leaf entry on a top-level entry to make it a child.

data.name: Use custom links for destinations that are not created automatically from installed apps.
data.value: Use custom links for destinations that are not created automatically from installed apps.

data.name: Leave empty for a top-level entry. Choose a parent to show the link under that group.
data.value: Leave empty for a top-level entry. Choose a parent to show the link under that group.

data.name: Favorites are personal shortcuts selected by each user from All modules. The Favorites topbar menu and favorite dashboard widgets use the same saved selections.
data.value: Favorites are personal shortcuts selected by each user from All modules. The Favorites topbar menu and favorite dashboard widgets use the same saved selections.

data.name: Admins control which entries can be selected as favorites by enabling or disabling them in All modules.
data.value: Admins control which entries can be selected as favorites by enabling or disabling them in All modules.

data.name: Update an entry shown in All modules and available as a favorite.
data.value: Update an entry shown in All modules and available as a favorite.

data.name: The selected parent would create a navigation cycle.
data.value: The selected parent would create a navigation cycle.

data.name: A navigation entry cannot be its own parent.
data.value: A navigation entry cannot be its own parent.

data.name: The layout update would create a navigation cycle.
data.value: The layout update would create a navigation cycle.

data.name: The admin area is organized around one OMP installation. Use Installation for desired topology, Import/export for portable objects, and Operations for HostAgent troubleshooting.
data.value: The admin area is organized around one OMP installation. Use Installation for desired topology, Import/export for portable objects, and Operations for HostAgent troubleshooting.

data.name: Import and export OMP installation objects. Use universal module packages for the most flexible transport format, or import individual module definitions, artifact packages, host configs, config overlays, and widgets.
data.value: Import and export OMP installation objects. Use universal module packages for the most flexible transport format, or import individual module definitions, artifact packages, host configs, config overlays, and widgets.

data.name: Upload one or more standard artifact package zips without changing any module definition.
data.value: Upload one or more standard artifact package zips without changing any module definition.

data.name: Export one installed artifact as a standard artifact package zip, including artifact-owned configuration files.
data.value: Export one installed artifact as a standard artifact package zip, including artifact-owned configuration files.

data.name: Artifact packages imported or replaced: {0}. Failed: {1}.
data.value: Artifact packages imported or replaced: {0}. Failed: {1}.

data.name: Start by choosing whether you want to import something into this OMP installation or export something from it.
data.value: Start by choosing whether you want to import something into this OMP installation or export something from it.

data.name: Add or update portable objects in this installation.
data.value: Add or update portable objects in this installation.

data.name: Download portable objects from this installation.
data.value: Download portable objects from this installation.

data.name: Choose one action. Only that form will be shown.
data.value: Choose one action. Only that form will be shown.

data.name: Choose import or export to continue.
data.value: Choose import or export to continue.

data.name: Import one outer zip that contains a module definition and artifact packages.
data.value: Import one outer zip that contains a module definition and artifact packages.

data.name: Import a module definition JSON together with one or more artifact package zips.
data.value: Import a module definition JSON together with one or more artifact package zips.

data.name: Import one or more artifact package zips without changing a module definition.
data.value: Import one or more artifact package zips without changing a module definition.

data.name: Import dashboard widget definitions from JSON.
data.value: Import dashboard widget definitions from JSON.

data.name: Import a module package already stored in the shared package library.
data.value: Import a module package already stored in the shared package library.

data.name: Export a module definition with the artifact packages it references.
data.value: Export a module definition with the artifact packages it references.

data.name: Export all widgets or widgets owned by one module.
data.value: Export all widgets or widgets owned by one module.

data.name: Use this when the module definition JSON and artifact packages are separate files.
data.value: Use this when the module definition JSON and artifact packages are separate files.

data.name: Export all widgets or one selected dashboard widget.
data.value: Export all widgets or one selected dashboard widget.

data.name: Export all widgets or choose one of the widgets available in this OMP installation.
data.value: Export all widgets or choose one of the widgets available in this OMP installation.

data.name: Inspect HostAgent app deployments, artifact provisioning history, and runtime identity checks.
data.value: Inspect HostAgent app deployments, artifact provisioning history, and runtime identity checks.

data.name: These rows show the latest HostAgent artifact download and verification result per host. The default view shows rows from the last 24 hours that may need attention.
data.value: These rows show the latest HostAgent artifact download and verification result per host. The default view shows rows from the last 24 hours that may need attention.

data.name: You have unsaved dashboard changes. Save them before leaving edit mode?
data.value: You have unsaved dashboard changes. Save them before leaving edit mode?

data.name: When this is turned off, the Portal navbar below the topbar is hidden.
data.value: When this is turned off, the Portal navbar below the topbar is hidden.

data.name: Unsaved local dashboard changes were found. Restore them?
data.value: Unsaved local dashboard changes were found. Restore them?

data.name: Dashboard changes could not be saved. Your local draft was kept.
data.value: Dashboard changes could not be saved. Your local draft was kept.

data.name: Your session appears to have expired. Sign in again before saving dashboard changes.
data.value: Your session appears to have expired. Sign in again before saving dashboard changes.

data.name: The server could not be reached. Your local dashboard draft was kept.
data.value: The server could not be reached. Your local dashboard draft was kept.

data.name: The selected file is too large for this upload surface.
data.value: The selected file is too large for this upload surface.

data.name: Select an image or GIF file.
data.value: Select an image or GIF file.

data.name: Could not update blank widget image library.
data.value: Could not update blank widget image library.

data.name: The selected image is too large.
data.value: The selected image is too large.

data.name: When this is turned off, the dashboard navbar below the topbar is hidden.
data.value: When this is turned off, the dashboard navbar below the topbar is hidden.

data.name: Hide title bar outside edit mode
data.value: Hide title bar outside edit mode

data.name: This resets your dashboard to the shared default layout. Continue?
data.value: This resets your dashboard to the shared default layout. Continue?

data.name: Reset to the shared default dashboard layout.
data.value: Reset to the shared default dashboard layout.

data.name: This removes all widgets from your dashboard. Continue?
data.value: This removes all widgets from your dashboard. Continue?

data.name: Clear your dashboard and keep it empty until you add widgets again.
data.value: Clear your dashboard and keep it empty until you add widgets again.

data.name: This replaces the shared default dashboard layout with your current dashboard layout. Continue?
data.value: This replaces the shared default dashboard layout with your current dashboard layout. Continue?

data.name: Users without a personal dashboard layout use the shared default layout.
data.value: Users without a personal dashboard layout use the shared default layout.

data.name: Create one universal zip with any mix of module definitions, artifact packages, host configs, config overlays, widgets, and widget runtime data.
data.value: Create one universal zip with any mix of module definitions, artifact packages, host configs, config overlays, widgets, and widget runtime data.

data.name: Include runtime data for selected widgets
data.value: Include runtime data for selected widgets

data.name: Create system-controlled banners for all users or selected roles. Users cannot dismiss banners.
data.value: Create system-controlled banners for all users or selected roles. Users cannot dismiss banners.

data.name: Expires at must be after starts at.
data.value: Expires at must be after starts at.

data.name: Review recent personal notifications for your OMP user.
data.value: Review recent personal notifications for your OMP user.

data.name: Copies the applied module definition and exportable installed artifacts into the shared package library so they can be imported later from Available package library.
data.value: Copies the applied module definition and exportable installed artifacts into the shared package library so they can be imported later from Available package library.

data.name: Exported module {0} {1} to the package library. Definition file: {2}. Artifacts exported: {3}. Skipped: {4}.
data.value: Exported module {0} {1} to the package library. Definition file: {2}. Artifacts exported: {3}. Skipped: {4}.

data.name: Start a direct chat or create a group chat with OMP users.
data.value: Start a direct chat or create a group chat with OMP users.

data.name: Use a PNG, JPEG, or WebP image up to 2 MB.
data.value: Use a PNG, JPEG, or WebP image up to 2 MB.

data.name: Import and export OMP installation objects with the universal module package format. Individual legacy package formats are intentionally kept out of this workflow.
data.value: Import and export OMP installation objects with the universal module package format. Individual legacy package formats are intentionally kept out of this workflow.

data.name: Use maintenance tasks to remove old platform data that is intentionally kept in multiple versions. Artifact cleanup keeps the newest registered versions per artifact identity and only deletes older versions that are not referenced by desired runtime configuration.
data.value: Use maintenance tasks to remove old platform data that is intentionally kept in multiple versions. Artifact cleanup keeps the newest registered versions per artifact identity and only deletes older versions that are not referenced by desired runtime configuration.

data.name: Deleted {0} artifact versions. Removed {1} payloads; {2} payloads were already missing. Queued {3} HostAgent cleanup jobs for {4} host cache entries.
data.value: Deleted {0} artifact versions. Removed {1} payloads; {2} payloads were already missing. Queued {3} HostAgent cleanup jobs for {4} host cache entries.

data.name: Deleted {0} artifact versions. Queued {1} HostAgent cleanup jobs for {2} artifact store entries and {3} host cache entries.
data.value: Deleted {0} artifact versions. Queued {1} HostAgent cleanup jobs for {2} artifact store entries and {3} host cache entries.

data.name: The cleanup groups artifacts by app, package type, and target. If a group contains more versions than the limit, the oldest registered versions become candidates. Candidates still referenced by desired app, worker, HostAgent, template, or host requirement rows are protected.
data.value: The cleanup groups artifacts by app, package type, and target. If a group contains more versions than the limit, the oldest registered versions become candidates. Candidates still referenced by desired app, worker, HostAgent, template, host requirement, current app deployment, or active HostAgent runtime rows are protected.

data.name: For example, 5 keeps the five newest registered versions for each artifact identity.
data.value: For example, 5 keeps the five newest registered versions for each artifact identity.

data.name: Payload folders are deleted under ArtifactUpload:ArtifactStoreRoot after the database rows have been removed.
data.value: Payload folders are deleted under ArtifactUpload:ArtifactStoreRoot after the database rows have been removed.

data.name: Central artifact store payloads are deleted by the first HostAgent that claims the global cleanup job. Portal does not need write access to ArtifactStore.
data.value: Central artifact store payloads are deleted by the first HostAgent that claims the global cleanup job. Portal does not need write access to ArtifactStore.

data.name: Delete all unreferenced old artifact versions shown in the preview?
data.value: Delete all unreferenced old artifact versions shown in the preview?

data.name: HostAgent jobs are queued in the database and picked up by the matching host during its next cycle.
data.value: HostAgent jobs are queued in the database and picked up by the matching host during its next cycle. Global jobs can be claimed by any HostAgent.

data.name: No HostAgent jobs have been queued yet.
data.value: No HostAgent jobs have been queued yet.

data.name: There are no unreferenced old artifact versions to delete.
data.value: There are no unreferenced old artifact versions to delete.

data.name: ArtifactUpload:ArtifactStoreRoot must point to an existing artifact store before artifact payloads can be cleaned up.
data.value: ArtifactUpload:ArtifactStoreRoot must point to an existing artifact store before artifact payloads can be cleaned up.

data.name: Use this standalone-compatible tool to create a universal OMP package zip from any mix of portable objects.
data.value: Use this standalone-compatible tool to create a universal OMP package zip from any mix of portable objects.

data.name: Only universal module packages are supported for import and export. Use the Universal package workflow.
data.value: Only universal module packages are supported for import and export. Use the Universal package workflow.

data.name: Drop universal package files here or click to browse.
data.value: Drop universal package files here or click to browse.

data.name: or click anywhere in this area to browse for one or more zip files.
data.value: or click anywhere in this area to browse for one or more zip files.

data.name: Select at least one universal module package zip.
data.value: Select at least one universal module package zip.

data.name: Select one universal module package zip to preview.
data.value: Select one universal module package zip to preview.

data.name: Preview supports one universal module package at a time. Use Import all to import multiple packages.
data.value: Preview supports one universal module package at a time. Use Import all to import multiple packages.

data.name: Imported {0} universal module packages. Imported/updated: {1}. Skipped: {2}. Failed: {3}.
data.value: Imported {0} universal module packages. Imported/updated: {1}. Skipped: {2}. Failed: {3}.

data.name: Showing {0} import detail rows from the completed import response. These details are rendered in the page and are not stored in cookies.
data.value: Showing {0} import detail rows from the completed import response. These details are rendered in the page and are not stored in cookies.

data.name: Showing first {0} detail rows; {1} additional rows were omitted to keep the browser cookie small.
data.value: Showing first {0} detail rows; {1} additional rows were omitted to keep the browser cookie small.

data.name: The import result was shortened to avoid an oversized browser cookie.
data.value: The import result was shortened to avoid an oversized browser cookie.

data.name: Queued HostAgent artifact retention cleanup job {0}. The HostAgent will recompute the candidates and perform both database and file cleanup. Current preview shows {1} deletable old versions out of {2} candidates with a keep limit of {3}.
data.value: Queued HostAgent artifact retention cleanup job {0}. The HostAgent will recompute the candidates and perform both database and file cleanup. Current preview shows {1} deletable old versions out of {2} candidates with a keep limit of {3}.

data.name: The HostAgent that claims the global retention job deletes database rows and central ArtifactStore payloads, then queues host-specific cache cleanup jobs. Portal only creates the job request.
data.value: The HostAgent that claims the global retention job deletes database rows and central ArtifactStore payloads, then queues host-specific cache cleanup jobs. Portal only creates the job request.

data.name: Queue a HostAgent job to delete unreferenced old artifact versions?
data.value: Queue a HostAgent job to delete unreferenced old artifact versions?

data.name: No iFrame app instances are available.
data.value: No iFrame app instances are available.

data.name: Select an iFrame app and URL to fill the target URL field. You can still edit the target URL manually.
data.value: Select an iFrame app and URL to fill the target URL field. You can still edit the target URL manually.

data.name: When checked, the target URL is generated from the selected iFrame URL when the entry is saved.
data.value: When checked, the target URL is generated from the selected iFrame URL when the entry is saved.

data.name: Select a valid iFrame app and URL.
data.value: Select a valid iFrame app and URL.

data.name: Optional host subfolder for host-specific objects
data.value: Optional host subfolder for host-specific objects

data.name: Choose any number of object files, then add them to the package list. Host-specific folders apply to host configs, config overlays, widgets, and widget runtime data.
data.value: Choose any number of object files, then add them to the package list. Host-specific folders apply to host configs, config overlays, widgets, and widget runtime data.

data.name: Package key and package version are required.
data.value: Package key and package version are required.

data.name: Manage iFrame URL targets and URL sets used by the iFrame module.
data.value: Manage iFrame URL targets and URL sets used by the iFrame module.

data.name: The iFrame URL tables are not installed.
data.value: The iFrame URL tables are not installed.

data.name: Add one target URL that can be used by normal iFrame URL sets or standalone portal entries.
data.value: Add one target URL that can be used by normal iFrame URL sets or standalone portal entries.

data.name: Optional comma or semicolon separated active role names.
data.value: Optional comma or semicolon separated active role names.

data.name: Create a URL set and choose which iFrame URLs it should contain.
data.value: Create a URL set and choose which iFrame URLs it should contain.

data.name: Use letters, numbers, dot, dash, colon or underscore.
data.value: Use letters, numbers, dot, dash, colon or underscore.

data.name: Create at least one iFrame URL before creating a URL set.
data.value: Create at least one iFrame URL before creating a URL set.

data.name: An iFrame URL set with this key already exists.
data.value: An iFrame URL set with this key already exists.

data.name: Select at least one iFrame URL.
data.value: Select at least one iFrame URL.

data.name: Shows per-host web app probes written by HostAgent. Portal health should return a non-success status when the app cannot serve real traffic.
data.value: Shows per-host web app probes written by HostAgent. Portal health should return a non-success status when the app cannot serve real traffic.

data.name: No web app health state has been recorded yet.
data.value: No web app health state has been recorded yet.

data.name: You are signed in with Windows/AD, but creating your own OMP account is disabled for this installation.
data.value: You are signed in with Windows/AD, but creating your own OMP account is disabled for this installation.

data.name: This sign-in is not eligible to create an OMP account automatically. Contact an administrator if you need an OMP account.
data.value: This sign-in is not eligible to create an OMP account automatically. Contact an administrator if you need an OMP account.

data.name: Roles assigned directly to this OMP user or to one of its linked AD accounts are shown here.
data.value: Roles assigned directly to this OMP user or to one of its linked AD accounts are shown here.

data.name: No roles are assigned directly to this user.
data.value: No roles are assigned directly to this user.

data.name: Profile image storage is unavailable. Contact an administrator.
data.value: Profile image storage is unavailable. Contact an administrator.

data.name: Profile image storage is not installed. Run core module SQL repair and try again.
data.value: Profile image storage is not installed. Run core module SQL repair and try again.

data.name: Choose OMP users from the checklist or paste AD users/groups as newline-separated text.
data.value: Choose OMP users from the checklist or paste AD users/groups as newline-separated text.

data.name: Enter one AD user per line. Linked AD accounts are assigned as their OMP user automatically.
data.value: Enter one AD user per line. Linked AD accounts are assigned as their OMP user automatically.

data.name: Enter one AD group SID or DOMAIN\\Group per line. Local group names are resolved to a SID when possible.
data.value: Enter one AD group SID or DOMAIN\\Group per line. Local group names are resolved to a SID when possible.

data.name: Bind OMP users, AD users or AD groups to the role.
data.value: Bind OMP users, AD users or AD groups to the role.

data.name: Select at least one principal to add.
data.value: Select at least one principal to add.

data.name: Skipped {0} duplicate or already assigned principal(s).
data.value: Skipped {0} duplicate or already assigned principal(s).

data.name: Imported universal module package {0}. Imported/updated: {1}. Skipped: {2}. Failed: {3}.
data.value: Imported universal module package {0}. Imported/updated: {1}. Skipped: {2}. Failed: {3}.

data.name: The detailed import result is no longer available. Run the import again if you need the row details.
data.value: The detailed import result is no longer available. Run the import again if you need the row details.

data.name: Select a host and HostAgent artifact before requesting an upgrade.
data.value: Select a host and HostAgent artifact before requesting an upgrade.

data.name: HostAgent upgrade was requested. The active HostAgent will install and start the selected version during its next cycle.
data.value: HostAgent upgrade was requested. The active HostAgent will install and start the selected version during its next cycle.

data.name: HostAgent desired version already matched the selected artifact, or the selected host/artifact was not valid.
data.value: HostAgent desired version already matched the selected artifact, or the selected host/artifact was not valid.

data.name: Select a host before requesting a web app health probe.
data.value: Select a host before requesting a web app health probe.

data.name: Web app health probe job {0} was queued.
data.value: Web app health probe job {0} was queued.

data.name: Select a host before requesting an application-pool recycle.
data.value: Select a host before requesting an application-pool recycle.

data.name: Web app application-pool recycle job {0} was queued.
data.value: Web app application-pool recycle job {0} was queued.

data.name: Select a host before requesting web app logs.
data.value: Select a host before requesting web app logs.

data.name: Web app log collection job {0} was queued.
data.value: Web app log collection job {0} was queued.
